headlines.title: Girl raped by boys spared jail tells BBC judge's decision was like 'rock in my face'
headlines.description: Speaking to the BBC’s Laura Kuenssberg, the girl, who is now 16, said: "What was the point in putting me through that?"

headlines.title: Suspect killed after opening fire on Secret Service near White House
headlines.description: Officials say a suspect approached a checkpoint near the building and opened fire, and that a bystander was wounded.

headlines.title: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
headlines.description: The Oreshnik missile, which reportedly travels over 10 times the speed of sound, was used, Russia has confirmed.

headlines.title: Midnight Sun-derland: Zara Larsson electrifies the crowd at Big Weekend
headlines.description: The Swedish pop star treated the crowds to back-to-back, high-energy pop bangers on day two of the festival.

headlines.title: Why the stakes are so high in Spurs and West Ham's relegation shootout
headlines.description: Why the stakes could not be higher in Tottenham and West Ham's relegation shootout.

headlines.title: It's like the Olympics - except steroids are allowed
headlines.description: The first ever Enhanced Games are taking place this weekend in Las Vegas, with big names, big money and much controversy.

headlines.title: Spurred on by the painful death of her dad, Natalie Cassidy trains as a carer
headlines.description: The actress's experience caring for her father inspired her to return to education in a new BBC show.

headlines.title: I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
headlines.description: Sunil Puniya was on his first sea job when a missile struck the oil tanker Skylight at the start of the Iran war.

headlines.title: 'Six eggs used to be £1' - why everyday essentials cost so much more now
headlines.description: Six supermarket brand eggs cost £1 in 2022. How much are they now, why have they gone up, and is anyone profiteering?

headlines.title: New James Bond game shows more vulnerable side to iconic British spy
headlines.description: The video game presents a young Bond before he has earned his "00" spy status.

headlines.title: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
headlines.description: Officials say the train had been transporting military personnel home for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'We'll axe tax on overtime' and 'Brolly hot, isn't it'
headlines.description: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage's pledge to scrap income tax on overtime is prominent across Sunday's papers.

headlines.title: Woman abused by babysitter as a child says compensation can't fix police injustice
headlines.description: Michaela Allen reported abuse at the age of seven, but police errors meant no-one faced justice.

headlines.title: East Africa wants to curb imports of used clothes. But it's not easy
headlines.description: Kenya, Uganda and Tanzania are major importers of used garments from the West and China.

headlines.title: Scrap tax on overtime hours, says Reform UK
headlines.description: The party says its proposed policy would cost £5bn a year and could be paid for through cuts to welfare.

headlines.title: Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast
headlines.description: The 39-year-old was fatally injured while swimming out at a shoal off the Cassowary Coast in Queensland.

headlines.title: In Japan, divorce splits parents from children. Could a law change end sole custody?
headlines.description: An amendment to Japan's Civil Code looks set to allow shared parenting for the first time.

headlines.title: Bride weds in dressing gown, slippers and rollers as 'it's what married life is like'
headlines.description: Ashleigh Stanley says the dressed-down ceremony gave her husband a "reality check" on married life.

headlines.title: 'I lost my best friend' - TV presenter says death of his dog was 'unique and profound'
headlines.description: The presenter says the loss of a pet can be as significant as human loss.

headlines.title: Police officer catches baby dropped from window of burning home
headlines.description: Body cam footage shows the moment a baby was thrown from a window and caught by an officer during a house fire.

headlines.title: Giant moon and UFO take over Chelsea streets
headlines.description: London's largest free flower festival returns to Chelsea with a cosmic "Out of this World" theme.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Could A Brexit Reset Mean A New Single Market? (Live At Hay Festival)
headlines.description: Newscast is live at Hay Festival and looking at the UK’s relationship with the EU.

headlines.title: Will Trump invade Cuba?
headlines.description: The US charges Cuba’s 94-year-old former president Raul Castro with murder.

headlines.title: Why the stakes are so high for Spurs and West Ham
headlines.description: Why the stakes could not be higher in Tottenham and West Ham's relegation shootout.

headlines.title: What next for heavyweight division after Usyk's win?
headlines.description: After Oleksandr Usyk beats Rico Verhoeven to retain his heavyweight world titles, who could he fight next?

headlines.title: 'Not mentally doable to stay like this' - Verstappen renews quit threat
headlines.description: Max Verstappen threatens to quit Formula 1 at the end of this season if planned changes to the engine regulations are blocked.

headlines.title: Russell edges Antonelli to claim Canada pole 'out of nowhere'
headlines.description: George Russell beats Mercedes team-mate Kimi Antonelli to take pole position at the Canadian Grand Prix.

headlines.title: Farewell to Salah, Liverpool's 'Egyptian king'
headlines.description: As Mohamed Salah's time at Liverpool comes to an end, a look at his legacy with insight from Jurgen Klopp, Ian Rush, Steven Gerrard and more.

headlines.title: Bordeaux inspired by McIlroy's Masters feat
headlines.description: From Rory McIlroy to mentality, Bordeaux are leading a golden era as France complete a Six Nations, Champions Cup and Challenge Cup clean sweep.
